mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above 6% triggers a negative labor market shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods with rising unemployment above 6%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 800k triggers a reduction in residential investment and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods with declining housing starts below 800k.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 110 triggers an increase in supply chain capacity and efficiency, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production above 110 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2% triggers a reduction in discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate below 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in employment above 137k triggers an increase in consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising employment above 137k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 11700 triggers a reduction in consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining real GDP below 11700 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 110 triggers an increase in supply chain capacity and efficiency, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production above 110 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
